title: AB1388 (San Diego County Sheriff)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: AB1388 (San Diego County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Per AB 1388, I am seeking the department's separation agreements made with officers. The records may be considered non-disclosure agreements or clean-record agreements.  These agreements are now void and must be disclosed under state law. https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=202520260AB1388 

Please provide me with copies of these agreements, on a rolling basis as records are ready for release, that cover 2012 to today's date. 

"This bill would additionally exempt agreements between an employing agency and a peace officer that, among other things, require the agency to destroy, remove, or conceal a record of a misconduct investigation. The bill would declare that its provisions are severable."

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents related to a government contract with OpenAI referencing the enterprise-wide deployment of a ChatGPT-powered AI assistant. Documents could include the contract and/or enterprise agreement between Massachusetts and OpenAI.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aarushi Sahejpal

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Aarushi,

You can find the OpenAI contract documents here:

https://www.commbuys.com/bso/external/purchaseorder/poSummary.sda?docId=PO-19-1080-OSD03-SRC01-17283&releaseNbr=31&parentUrl=close

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thank you,

Michelle
617-447-1754

title: Florida DJJ Youth Pay to Stay Policy

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida DJJ Youth Pay to Stay Policy
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of fees and debt for incarcerated youth (i.e., juvenile):

a) All current policies and procedures regarding medical and room & board costs or fees for which incarcerated youth (i.e. juvenile) and/or their parents or legal guardians are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc).

b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding medical copays and room and board fees paid to or owed to this department/office or any facility/agency under its jurisdiction in FY2024. Please include all relevant data regarding medical and room & board costs, including the number of youths (i.e., juveniles) charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed in FY2024.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Jenny Wadhwa

title: AB1388 (Burbank Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: AB1388 (Burbank Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Per AB 1388, I am seeking the department's separation agreements made with officers. The records may be considered non-disclosure agreements or clean-record agreements.  These agreements are now void and must be disclosed under state law. https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=202520260AB1388 

Please provide me with copies of these agreements, on a rolling basis as records are ready for release, that cover 2012 to today's date. 

"This bill would additionally exempt agreements between an employing agency and a peace officer that, among other things, require the agency to destroy, remove, or conceal a record of a misconduct investigation. The bill would declare that its provisions are severable."

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: Public Records Request
communications.communication:
Dear Joey Scott,

Please see the attached documents in response to your Public Records Request for separation agreements that cover 2012 to date.

Thank you,

title: Request to Marines for all 2025 film and TV collaborations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Marines for all 2025 film and TV collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films and television productions that the US Marines has supported, in any capacity whatsoever, during the year 2025. Such a list, full or partial, maybe include, but should not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports or similar reports. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Your verification code
communications.communication: Your confirmation code is 325346

communications.subject: Your FOIA Request 2026-001849
communications.communication:
Mr. Carollo - This is to acknowledge the receipt of your requests and to let you know that I am the analyst assigned for processing.  If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me or our office. 

Please be aware that due to the government shutdown we are still ‘catching up’ and there may be some significant delays in receiving a response.  Please note that we are doing our best and are working diligently to complete all incoming requests. 

Please also be aware that, whenever a FOIA requester has not received a determination within 20 working days from the date a request is perfected, they are entitled to challenge the delay.  Appeals may be submitted within the SecureRelease online portal at www.securerelease.us or by writing to the Department of the Navy, Office of the Judge Advocate General (Code 14), 1322 Patterson Ave SE, Suite 3000, Washington Navy Yard, DC 20374-5066.  If you prefer, you can also submit an appeal via e-mail to OJAG-C14-FOIA-PA-ADMINAPPEALS@us.navy.mil.  Your appeal, if any, should include a statement indicating why you believe it should be granted. 

You also have the right to seek assistance and/or dispute resolution services from the Department of the Navy FOIA Public Liaison, Mr. Christopher Julka, at (703) 697-0031 or christopher.a.julka.civ@us.navy.mil.  You may also contact the Office of Government Information Services for assistance at ogis@nara.gov or (877) 684-6448. 

Sincerely, 

Deanna James 
FOIA Analyst

communications.subject: Your FOIA Request 2026-USMCFOIA-001849

Mr. Carollo – Attached, please see our response to your FOIA request.  Any questions, please feel free to contact our office. 

Sincerely, 

Deanna James 
FOIA Analyst/Team Lead 

Commandant of the Marine Corps (ARSF) 
Headquarters US Marine Corps 
3000 Marine Corps Pentagon 
Washington DC 20350-3000

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Caldwell County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Caldwell County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. title: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (Tennessee) (University of Tennessee, Knoxville)

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (Tennessee) (University of Tennessee, Knoxville)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) The most recent contract, governing, base, or master license agreement with William S. Hein & Co. relating to the licensing of the “HeinOnline” database;
2) All related terms and conditions, terms of service, amendments, addenda, appendices, attachments, product terms, MOUs, MOAs, data processing agreements, and/or letters of intent; and
3) All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to the contractual relations with William S. Hein & Co. for the “HeinOnline” database.

Please do not limit your search to strictly direct agreements or renewals between the institution and William S. Hein & Co., but include any agreements and related documentation through consortia such as Tenn-Share, the Tennessee Academic Library Cooperative (TALC), or Lyrasis.

Responsive materials include all contracts, agreements, amendments, appendices, invoices, and any other documents listed above that incorporate language governing terms of use of the “HeinOnline” database.

We are an alliance of academic research librarians conducting critical research on library vendor contract terminology related to data privacy to identify and expose significant disparities in language between vendors and institutions. This research is essential for advancing national awareness and advocacy around library patron data privacy rights. We would be grateful for your cooperation in supporting this important initiative.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Michele Gibney.

communications.subject: UTS-446-2025 - Pre-payment required
communications.communication:
Kent Hoover, documents that may be responsive to your request have been identified. 
  
 The Tennessee Public Records Act allows the University to charge for the labor involved in gathering and producing documents, and to request a prepayment of fifty (50) percent of the estimated labor charge, prior to beginning the process. 
  
 The TPRA stipulates that the first hour of highest-compensated labor be provided at no charge. Therefore, the estimated labor cost for this request is 8.0 hours of labor at $22.57 per hour, for a total of $180.56. 
  
 In order to proceed, you will need to pay $90.28, which is 50% of the estimated total. 
  
 Payment can be made with debit/credit card here. 
  
 Once the University receives payment, the process of gathering the records will begin. 
  
 When the work is complete, the actual labor that was expended in the request will be calculated, and you will be notified of any remaining labor cost. 
  
 Please advise us how you wish to proceed. 
  
 Sincerely, 
  
 Charles Primm
Public Records Manager
UT System Administration
865-974-5180

I attempted to send the $90.28 though MuckRock but am unsure if the payment was successful. Can you confirm whether you have received any notification that a payment is being processed?

Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: Tennessee Public Records Act Request #UTS-446-2025
communications.communication:
Dear Charles,

Just FYI that an admin at Muckrock responded to my request for assistance and let me know that the payment is being processed. They said, "It looks like your payment did go through successfully. Our team is confirming the correct check address before sending out payment, which should take maybe another day at most."

We will look forward to your next communication regarding the document request and would appreciate a confirmation of payment once you receive it.

Thank you!

communications.subject: Welcome to the City of Chicago Request Center!
communications.communication:
Dear Jacqueline Watkins, 
 
 
Thank you for registering with the City of Chicago Public Records Portal. 
If you did not submit your request through the public portal but are receiving this e-mail, we are excited to share with you that you can now submit FOIA requests through a newly implemented online request system. While you will still be allowed to email your FOIA requests, creating an account and submitting your request online will allow you to check the online portal for the status of requests. You will also receive an email when the records are ready to be downloaded.

title: Moonlight Maze (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Moonlight Maze (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request the first thousand pages of records that your agency has on Moonlight Maze. Please keep in mind the amount of information on Moonlight Maze that has been publicly acknowledged by the US government, including that the Russian hacking group Turla is behind the Moonlight Maze cyberattack.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Mitch Poran

# Non-Individual FOIA Request

Request Information

To Whom It May Concern: Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby
request the following records: I request the first thousand pages of records
that your agency has on Moonlight Maze. Please keep in mind the amount of
information on Moonlight Maze that has been publicly acknowledged by the US
government, including that the Russian hacking group Turla is behind the
Moonlight Maze cyberattack. The requested documents will be made available to
the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial
purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would
inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would
prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or
CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this
matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20
business days, as the statute requires. Sincerely, Mitch Poran Upload
documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2211829/

**

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Harnett County Sheriff)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Harnett County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: RE: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Harnett County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Mr. Sinyangwe,

I am writing to confirm the receipt of your public records request.  I am forwarding this request to our attorney as part of the internal workflow for these requests.

Kindly,

Jeffrey Armstrong
Captain / Accreditation Manager
Professional Standards Division
910-893-9111 (Main)
910-893-0104 (Direct)
910-514-5496 (Mobile)
910-893-6450 (Fax)
www.harnettsheriff.com<http://www.harnettsheriff.com/>

communications.subject: RE: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Harnett County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following North Carolina Public Records Law request, copied below, and originally submitted on Jan. 28, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Harnett County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Samuel Sinyangwe,

Good Morning.  We are currently working on your inquiry to pull the data you have requested.  I do not have an expected production date at this time as the agency is still processing the request for which you have made.  I have copied our attorney on this email as part of our process is their review.

Thank you,
Captain Armstrong

Good morning,

Attached are the Harnett County Sheriff's Office reports responsive to your request. Please note, in the report for suspect age,  entry #55 for the year 2025 reports a suspect as being "<10". This is a data entry error and the suspects date of birth is actually 10/24/1984. 

Thank you.

title: Ronald A. Moore report

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Ronald A. Moore report
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

-I am requesting the full Ronald Moore interview conducted 4/06/1989 with regards to his movements December 5 through 8, 1978.  The previous response to this request was heavily redacted. I am requesting the report to not be redacted. I am also requesting the dates of any interviews conducted with Ronald Moore prior to 4/06/1989.
-I am requesting all statements made by David Neal Cohn, John Kelly, Michael Bruce and Steve Frechette  pertaining to the night of December 5th into the morning of December 6th 1978 in which the individuals made statements to their roles in bringing Theresa Corley to the Presidential Arms in Franklin MA. 
-I am requesting the documentation regarding Margaret Blaine bringing forth a letter from Donald Moore Sr. on or around the time of  his death

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

GERALDINE HOUDE

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Ronald A. Moore report
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Houde,

This letter responds to your December 29, 2025 request submitted pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, M.G.L. c. 66, § 10.
After a diligent review, the Bellingham Police Department responds as follows:

________________________________
Ronald Moore Interview (April 6, 1989) and Prior Interviews
With respect to the interview of Ronald Moore conducted on April 6, 1989, the Department notes that this report was generated by the Massachusetts State Police. Although a copy exists within this Department’s historical case file for reference purposes, the Bellingham Police Department is not the custodian of that record for purposes of the Public Records Law.
The Department possesses no records of any interviews of Ronald Moore conducted prior to April 6, 1989.
Additionally, the interview report relates to an open and unsolved homicide investigation. Disclosure of unredacted investigative materials would prejudice the possibility of effective law enforcement and is therefore exempt from disclosure pursuant to M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(f). The Public Records Law does not require an agency to release records in an unredacted form where lawful exemptions apply.
________________________________
Statements of David Neal Cohn, John Kelly, Michael Bruce, and Steve Frechette
The requested statements were generated by the Massachusetts State Police as part of a multi-agency investigation involving both an open and unsolved homicide and allegations of sexual assault occurring in Franklin, Massachusetts. Although copies may exist within this Department’s historical files for reference purposes, the records were not created by, nor are they maintained by, the Bellingham Police Department as its own public records. Accordingly, this Department is not the custodian of those records for purposes of the Public Records Law.
Further, the requested materials constitute witness statements and investigative records relating to an open homicide investigation and allegations of sexual assault. Disclosure would prejudice the possibility of effective law enforcement and is therefore exempt pursuant to M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(f).
Additionally, the statements contain highly sensitive personal information concerning alleged sexual assault, witnesses, and third parties. Disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy and is independently exempt from disclosure pursuant to M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(c).
________________________________
Documentation Regarding a Letter from Donald Moore Sr.
We do not have a copy of the letter that Donald SR. wrote. We only have a letter/report/memo from State Police Trooper Brian Brooks documenting his conversation with Margaret Blaine about the letter.
This document forms part of an active and unsolved homicide investigative file and was not authored by the Bellingham Police Department. Disclosure would reveal non-public investigative information and context and would prejudice the possibility of effective law enforcement. Accordingly, the document is exempt from disclosure pursuant to M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(f).
________________________________
Format and Accessibility
All responsive materials in the Department’s possession exist solely in paper form. The Public Records Law does not require the Department to convert historical paper records into electronic, OCR, or screen-reader-accessible formats, nor does it require the creation of new records in response to a request.
________________________________
Prior and Future Access
The Department notes that, in recognition of your status as a family member of the victim, you have previously been permitted to review the investigative case file in person. This accommodation exceeds the requirements of the Public Records Law.
If you wish to review the case file again, you may contact Detective Sergeant Marston to request an appointment, subject to availability and appropriate supervision.
________________________________
For records generated and maintained by the Massachusetts State Police,  you may wish to direct your request directly to that agency, or through your Victim Witness Advocate.
If you believe this response is in error, you may appeal to the Supervisor of Public Records pursuant to 950 CMR 32.08.


Sincerely,

Marianne Mahar
Administrative Assistant
Bellingham Police Department
Ph# 508-657-2872
Fax# 508-966-4669

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Ronald A. Moore report
communications.communication:
Thank you for your response to my FOIA request. I would like to appeal your decision based on the age of this case, 47 years and that a FOIA request in 2022 resulted in then Bellingham MA police Chief Gerard Daigle providing a redacted interview after the request and appeal were reviewed by the Supervisor of Records.
M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(f does not apply in this case. There has been a significant passage of time. Ronald Moore and Paul Thibodeau are both deceased. The information related to the individuals in the interview is widely known. There is no unwarranted invasion of personal privacy given the context of the unsolved capitol crime and the age of the case. There could potentially be a public benefit in releasing the information to potentially solving the crime and revealing the truth. Release of the statement may prompt others with information to come forward. 
There were investigatory failures in the initial stages of the investigation demonstrated by the fact the Ronald Moore presented himself to the Bellingham Police Department on December 8th, 1978 and inquired if it was "Theresa Corley's body on the side of the highway." According to the dispatcher at the time there was no reason Ronald Moore would have had that information since police officers had not gotten to the scene and the information on the discovery of the body was not widely broadcasted. It took 11 years for Ronald Moore to be interviewed by state investigators.  Bellingham PD has attested to in this FOIA request, that there are no other interviews of Ronald Moore. However, interviews were conducted by former Lt. Kevin Ranieri. Lt Ranieri has stated that "Ronnie would get picked up often" and at that point in time he wanted to "talk about Theresa Corley."It would seem if you have no documentation of those encounters then there was a lapse in investigatory effort and knowledge of such would be in the best interests of the public. 
I formally rescind the FOIA request of the individuals at the Presidential Arms, John Kelly, Steve Frechette, Michael Bruce and David Cohn. 
I formally rescind the FOIA request for the letter from Donald Moore Sr. given to Margaret Blaine.

communications.subject: FW: SPR26/0251 Appeal Acknowledgement- Additional Information from Bellingham PD
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

This is the response that went to the SPR. Please see below and the attached.

Thank you,

Marianne Mahar
Administrative Assistant
Bellingham Police Department
Ph# 508-657-2872
Fax# 508-966-4669





Good Afternoon,

I am writing briefly to provide additional context in support of the Department’s attached response.
Since I assumed command of the Bellingham Police Department in 2023, this matter has remained an active and ongoing investigation. During this period, the Department has continued investigative activity, including the collection of DNA from persons of interest or individuals potentially connected to persons of interest, receipt and follow-up on both telephone and in-person tips, preparation of investigative summaries, and interviews of potential witnesses.
In addition, the requestor has previously been afforded unfettered, supervised access to review the materials maintained by the Bellingham Police Department in connection with this case. That accommodation was provided in good faith and exceeded the Department’s obligations under the Public Records Law.
The Department remains mindful of the need to protect the integrity of the investigation and the safety and privacy of third parties. Based on the Department’s experience in this matter, disclosure of investigative materials that identify or could reasonably lead to the identification of individuals associated with the investigation presents a heightened risk of undue harassment or interference, and would jeopardize ongoing investigative efforts.
For these reasons, and as outlined in the attached response, the Department maintains that the requested records are properly withheld under the applicable exemptions.
Thank you for your consideration. Please feel free to contact me should you require any additional information.
Respectfully,
Ken Fitzgerald, MPA
Chief of Police
Bellingham Police Department
30 Blackstone Street • Bellingham, MA 02019

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Alachua County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Alachua County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: RE: [PRR 2526-102] Washington Public Records Act Request: Verra Special Needs Busing (Seattle Public Schools)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Below is the info from staff:


  *   We have contracted with two companies to provide bus service for students with special needs, and at this time the number of buses we utilize for that purpose is 217.  None of these are equipped with stop arm cameras as we are still working out the details of any such arrangement with the King County Sheriff’s Office.
  *   That said, we have made progress on this project and will be installing the cameras soon and that they will be installed on all the buses we use with First Student and Zum
  *   Our contractors currently utilize 127 Gen Ed buses and Verra is active installing cameras as we speak, to be completed within the next month.


The District does not intend to further address this request and the applicable statute of limitations for judicial review of the District’s decision will now begin to run.  If you feel we have misinterpreted the request or have any questions, or seek additional records, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

Thank you,


[SPS brandmark]
Randall D. Enlow (he/him)

Seattle Public Schools

Your request was received on January 29, 2026 and assigned this reference number: S003858-012926. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2202085/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to respond, and to identify and determine the number of records and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The California Public Records Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below. You will receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Sincerely, 
County of Nevada Staff

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2202085/” The County of Nevada has reviewed its files and has located responsive records to your request. While our records retention policy only requires us to maintain use of force records for two years, I did locate records dating back to 2019. Due to the volume of records, we estimate needing up to 14 days to redact records. Newer records are stored in a similar manner requested. Older records are not, so full use of force reports with redaction will be provided. I anticipate a records release to you by or before Feb. 24. If you have any questions or need additional information, please feel free to contact our office at (530) 265-1471. Sincerely, Ashley Quadros Nevada County Sheriff's Office
